compiled_state.title: As a developer, ticket scan operations exclude archived tickets by default
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: Add archived-ticket exclusion as the default behavior for all ticket scan operations, with an opt-in --include-archived flag for commands that need it.
**Why**: Archived tickets are irrelevant to active workflow operations (close, link, deps) but currently slow down every scan proportionally. Excluding them reduces the effective ticket count for all downstream operations.
**Scope**:
- IN: Adding exclude_archived=True to all scan callers except ticket show; adding --include-archived flag to ticket list and ticket deps; producing clear error messages when a command targets an archived ticket directly
- OUT: Changing the reducer's reduce_all_tickets() default parameter value (callers opt in, not a global default change); modifying ticket show behavior

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, all ticket scan operations except ticket show skip directories whose reduced state has archived: true
  ← Satisfies: "All ticket scan operations except ticket show exclude archived tickets by default"
- When this story is complete, ticket list and ticket deps accept --include-archived to override the default exclusion
  ← Satisfies: "Commands that search/list/query tickets accept --include-archived to override the default exclusion"
- When this story is complete, when a command encounters an archived ticket as a direct target (not via scan), it produces a clear error message suggesting --include-archived
  ← Satisfies: "All ticket scan operations except ticket show exclude archived tickets by default"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Transitive blocker traversal must NOT skip archived tickets in the middle of dependency chains — only skip as scan entry points
- [Testing] Test with archived tickets present — exclusion logic is invisible on fresh trackers with no archived tickets
- [Testing] Backward compatibility: --include-archived must be documented in CLI help
- [Reliability] ticket-graph.py compute_archive_eligible() explicitly passes exclude_archived=False because it must see all tickets to determine archival eligibility. The archived-exclusion default must not break this call path.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. "High confidence" means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Story complete: all ticket scan operations except ticket show exclude archived by default. ticket list and ticket deps accept --include-archived. Clear error for archived direct target. All tests pass.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
